reason: Verb and classifier senses both exist; at HSK1 the core directional-verb sense 'to go out' is what a learner needs, not the opera/play classifier.
Primary HSK1 use is the age counter ('X岁' = X years old); the bare 'year' sense is secondary for beginners.
Surname Gao and 'loud/honorific' senses set aside; the adjective 'high/tall' is the HSK1 sense.
Many senses (advance, submit, admit, math base, building classifier); HSK1 learner needs the basic motion verb 'to enter'.
Surname and 'grind/polish/inlay/interlock' senses set aside; HSK1 needs the 'wrong; mistake' sense (also 不错 = not bad).
Both 'ball (object)' and 'ball game/match' senses exist; chose the concrete object sense primary with sport tag, as that is the HSK1 anchor (球 in 足球/篮球).
Highly polysemous (dynasty name, 'first/primary', math variable, surname), but at HSK1 with this freq the learner needs the everyday money sense.
cedict spans many senses (in vain, plain, to state, vernacular), but the HSK1 primary sense is the color.
Means both 'written character' and 'word/letter'; at HSK1 the core sense is the Chinese character one writes/reads.
cedict lists teacher/husband/(dialect) doctor too, but the modern primary HSK1 sense is the polite address 'mister/sir'.

cedict lists a (slang) 'prostitute' sense, but for HSK-1 the primary, level-appropriate sense is the polite address 'miss / young lady'. The slang sense is regionally loaded and not what a beginner needs as the card meaning.
cedict marks it as a bound form with extra senses (politics 'right of center', archaic 'west', literary 'precedence'). For HSK-1 the spatial 'right-hand side' is the only sense a learner needs.
Multiple senses (meat, fruit pulp, flabby, Kangxi radical). Primary HSK-1 sense is the food noun 'meat'.
Cedict lists many senses incl. 'to beat/hit' (variant) and 'deceased father', but the HSK-1 learning sense is the exam/test verb.
chosen: to take an exam; to test

reason: Two registers: aesthetic 'good-looking/nice' vs. figurative 'embarrassed; humiliated'. HSK-1 learner needs the literal aesthetic sense.
Cedict adds a slang sense (Shadowsocks proxy provider); irrelevant at HSK-1. Primary literal sense kept.

cedict spans milk, breast, to breastfeed, and mother/granny. For HSK1 the core sense a learner needs is the noun 'milk' (as in 牛奶), with 'breast' as the literal body sense; verb and 'mother' senses set aside.

cedict spans degree sense ('not very') and frequency sense ('not often'); at HSK1 the degree-adverb sense before adjectives is primary, with the frequency sense as a close secondary kept in the gloss.
cedict adds an Internet-slang sense ('previous poster in a thread'); irrelevant at HSK1, so the literal spatial sense is chosen.
literal 'half a day' vs idiomatic 'a long while'; both are common and short, so kept together with the literal sense first.

Cedict spans 'have spare time / free from work' and the reassurance sense. The HSK-1 colloquial primary use is the reassurance reply ('it's nothing / never mind'), so I chose that over the 'be free/have spare time' sense.
alternatives: to have spare time; be free from work

reason: Cedict lists 'notebook', 'book', 'edition', and a slang 'dōjinshi (often erotic)' sense. At HSK-1 the needed sense is the everyday school notebook; the slang/edition senses are inappropriate and irrelevant for a beginner card.
Means 'girl' generally; can read as young girl or young woman/girlfriend in context. Chose neutral 'девочка; девушка / girl' as the level-appropriate primary sense.
Polysemous and toneform-split. At HSK 2 (wéi) the primary need is the copula/verb sense 'to be/act as'; the 'for/because of' preposition is the wèi reading. Chose wéi verb sense per given pinyin.
Spans causative 'let/make sb do', 'yield/give way', and passive 'by'. Chose the core HSK-2 causative sense learners meet first.
chosen: to let; to make sb do

reason: Input lists surname, 'towards', 'support/side with', etc. Chose the HSK-2 directional preposition 'towards/facing' as primary.
Input pinyin given as 'gōu' (variant of 勾), but the HSK-2 word 句 is jù 'sentence/measure word for phrases'. Corrected pinyin to jù and glossed the sentence/classifier sense.
Core sense 'to sell' clearly primary; figurative 'betray / show off' set aside as advanced.
Highly polysemous (province abbr., sinister, illegal, to hack), but the HSK-2 core sense is the color.
Spans gas/air, weather, anger, and qi; at HSK-2 the foundational concrete sense is air/gas (anger appears mainly in compounds like 生气).
Means 'eye' but also functions as a classifier (wells, etc.) and 'small hole'; learner needs the body-part sense first.
Many senses (meaning, intention, interest/fun, token of goodwill); the primary HSK-2 sense learners meet is 'meaning'.
Broad verb (go through, connect, be expert, communicate) plus classifier uses; chose the core 'pass through / connect' sense over the classifier and 'expert' senses.
Verb/noun/classifier all common; for HSK2 the 'row/line up' sense (queue, row of seats) is the everyday meaning a learner needs over 'platoon' or 'rehearse'.
chosen: ряд; выстраивать в ряд (row; line up)

reason: Read wéi here. Same char also means 'to feed' (wèi), but the phone-greeting/hey sense is the primary HSK2 use and matches the given pinyin reading.
Animal/food sense is the HSK2 meaning; slang 'prostitute' sense set aside as inappropriate for a beginner card.
Multiple senses (to seal / to confer / classifier for letters). Chose the two most useful for a learner: the verb 'seal' and the very common classifier for letters.
chosen: запечатать; счётн. для писем (to seal; cl. for letters)

reason: Adjective and verb senses span 'urgent/anxious' and 'to make anxious'; for HSK 2 the primary need is the predicative adjective 'urgent / in a hurry / anxious'.
Two readings: gāozhōng 'senior high school' (noun) vs gāozhòng 'to pass an exam brilliantly'. The school sense is the HSK-2 vocabulary item.
Common modern verb sense 'to practice/train' vs literary noun 'white silk'; learner needs the verb.
alternatives: to boil and scour raw silk

reason: cedict lists 'adult' and an honorific 'title of respect toward superiors'; at HSK 2 the everyday sense is the one learners need.
alternatives: title of respect toward superiors (your honor / милорд)

reason: Spans 'topic/subject', 'exam question/problem', and verb 'to inscribe'. At HSK 2 the dominant classroom sense is an exam/homework question or problem.
cedict spans good deed, charity, happy occasion, a sarcastic 'fine thing indeed', and a funeral rite. Chose the neutral primary sense for the level.
chosen: good deed; good thing / доброе дело

reason: cedict has 'head of household/patriarch' and 'parent or guardian'. In modern HSK usage 家长 overwhelmingly means a pupil's parent/guardian (school context).
Cedict leads with 'variant of' noise; the real learner sense is the physical verb. Chose the core action meaning.
Dataset glosses inherit '接 = to accept/take', but the fixed compound 接下来 is an adverbial 'next/then/what follows'. Chose the compound's actual meaning over the morpheme gloss.
alternatives: to accept; to take (literal morpheme reading, wrong for the compound)

reason: Spans 'except for/besides' and 'outside of/beyond'. Both are common at HSK2; kept both close senses in the label.
alternatives: on the other side of; beyond (spatial only)

alternatives: go on a journey / be away from home

reason: Ranges over general 'way of doing / method / practice' and the cooking-specific 'recipe'. Kept the general sense primary with 'recipe' appended since both are frequent at this level.
cedict lists many senses (ripe/cooked/familiar/skilled/deep sleep); at HSK2 the everyday cluster is ripe-cooked-familiar, which I glossed together rather than picking one isolated sense.
Two distinct senses: noun 'camera' (abbr. of 照相机) vs. adverbial 'at the opportune moment'. For an HSK2 learner the concrete noun 'camera' is the needed primary sense.
alternatives: при удобном случае (at the opportune moment)

reason: Has a neutral 'good person' sense and a pejorative 'people-pleaser at expense of principle' sense; the literal positive sense is the HSK2 primary one.
cedict spans adverb 'greatly' and dialect kinship 'dad/uncle'. At HSK2 the productive adverbial sense (大大提高 etc.) is what a learner needs; kinship is regional/dialectal.

Head of any institution ending in 院; common referents are dean/president/director. Chose the broad institutional-head sense covering university dean/hospital director.

cedict spans 'boot up computer', 'start an engine', and 'begin filming'. For HSK2 the everyday sense is powering on a device (phone/computer), so led with that and dropped the filming/engine senses.

Spans kin/relative, intimate, in-person, and 'to kiss', plus internet-slang 'dear'. For HSK3 the family/relative sense plus the common verb 'to kiss' best triangulate the character.
Senses range over to shine/illuminate, according to, to reflect, and to take a photo. Picked shine + take-photo as the two most needed everyday senses at this level.

reason: Bound form covering whole/entire/exactly vs. tidy/in order vs. colloquial 'to fix/mess with sb'. Chose the numeric 'whole; exactly' sense most useful at HSK3.
Two readings: kōng (empty/sky) and kòng (free time/vacancy). Input gives kōng; chose empty/sky as primary, leaving 'free time' (kòng) aside.

reason: Spans verb 'replace/act on behalf of' and noun 'generation/era'; both are core HSK-3 senses, so kept a compact pairing rather than dropping one.
Both the everyday noun 'certificate/ID' and the verb 'to prove' are needed; led with the noun sense most salient to learners (e.g. 身份证).

to overtake / hurry / drive (cattle) / by (a time); the primary HSK3 sense is hurrying to catch something.

Surname/dynasty-name senses and verbal 'even sth out' set aside; at HSK3 the core adjectival sense 'neat/even/all together' is what a learner needs.

Spans the verbal 'to score (a point)' and the nominal 'score/rating/grade'. At HSK3 the sports/test-scoring verb sense plus its noun 'score' is the primary need; chose to cover both since they share the core sense.
chosen: to score; score (points in a game/test)

reason: Has a literal directional 'come up/approach' sense and an abstract verb-complement use indicating success. At HSK3 the concrete directional motion sense is what learners need.
alternatives: verb complement indicating success (答上来 etc.)

reason: cedict spans 'brains/mind' and figurative 'leader/boss'. At HSK3 the learner needs the literal 'mind/brains' sense, which is far more frequent in everyday usage.

Senses split between 'to correspond (by letter)' and modern technical 'telecommunication'. Chose the broad 'communication/correspondence' sense covering both; ru notes 'переписка' for the letter sense.

cedict spans two distinct sense-clusters: adverbial 'coincidentally/opportunely' and adjectival 'skillful/clever'. At HSK-3 the adverbial 'as it happens / by coincidence' sense (e.g. 正巧, 碰巧) is what learners meet first, but 'skillful' (灵巧, 巧手) is common too, so I kept both in the gloss with the coincidental sense primary.
Can be a noun ('ranking, place in a list') or a verb ('to rank Nth'). Noun sense is the more frequent learner-facing meaning, so primary; verb sense appended.
Spans 'everyone present/whole audience' and 'whole duration of a match'. At HSK3 the spatial/people sense (everyone in the venue) is the more learnable primary; kept duration aside.
chosen: весь зал; все присутствующие (whole audience / all present)

reason: Multiple senses: approximate time ('around'), 'from beginning to end', and spatial 'front and rear'. The approximate-time/extent sense is most common in HSK3 usage; spatial front-rear kept as secondary.
chosen: примерно; от начала до конца (around; from start to end)

reason: cedict lists 'location below' and 'afterwards'. Spatial 'below/underneath' is the dominant and level-appropriate sense; temporal 'afterwards' set aside.
Means both 'sign/plate' and 'brand/trademark'. The 'brand' sense is the more frequent everyday usage; kept 'sign' as secondary in the gloss.
Adjective sense 'amusing/funny' vs loanword noun 'cola'. At HSK3 with everyday vocabulary, the drink 'cola' is the sense a learner needs and recognizes.
Spans literal 'to untie/undo' and figurative 'to solve (a mystery)'. Kept both close senses since they triangulate the core 'open up/loosen' meaning; literal listed first as primary for this level.

cedict spans noun (aspiration/ambition) and verb (to volunteer). At HSK-3 the noun sense 'aspiration/wish' is primary (e.g. 志愿 as personal aspiration, also 高考志愿 for school choices), but the volunteer verb sense is common too; kept both glosses with noun first.
literal 'push open (a door)' vs figurative 'reject/decline'. The literal physical sense is the primary one a learner needs at HSK-3; included the closely related 'push away'.

Spans a verb sense (to divide into groups) and noun senses (subgroup; networking packet). For HSK 3 the everyday verb/action sense 'divide into groups' is the primary one a learner needs; the technical 'packet' sense is specialist.

Two readings: suǒcháng = one's strong point / what one is good at; suǒzhǎng = head of an institute. The institutional-title reading (suǒzhǎng) is the more concrete, frequent learner sense and matches the occupation framing; chose it and set pinyin accordingly.

Spans lunar-calendar first day vs. junior-middle-school grade 7. Chose the lunar-month/New Year sense as the primary one a learner meets first, especially given 年初/date context; the school-grade sense is set aside.
chosen: first day of lunar month; lunar New Year's Day
alternatives: first year of junior middle school (grade 7)

reason: Cedict only redirects to 老头子. Authored the underlying sense directly: a colloquial term for an old man (can be affectionate or dismissive). Marked register as coll.

cedict lists 'wife and children' (qīzǐ) and 'wife' (qīzi). At HSK4 the standard sense is 'wife' (read qīzi). Chose 'wife' as the primary learner sense; the literary 'wife and children' reading is set aside.
Multi-sense bound morpheme: 'head/chief', 'first', and a classifier for poems/songs. At HSK4 the classifier use (一首歌) plus 'first/chief' is most needed; packed both into the label.
Spans 'office/bureau', 'situation/state', and classifier for games. Picked the two most useful HSK4 senses (bureau, situation) plus the classifier; dropped 'narrow/cramped'.
Spans 'wheel', 'to take turns', and classifier for rounds/recurring events. Chose wheel + take-turns + round classifier as the core HSK4 senses; dropped 'steamship'.

Spans 'plate/tray/dish', verb 'to coil/check', finance 'market', and a classifier. Chose the concrete primary noun 'plate/tray/dish' plus its classifier use for HSK4; set aside the verb and finance senses.
Spans adjective 'strange/odd', verb 'to blame', and colloquial 'rather/quite'. Chose 'strange' + 'to blame' as the two core HSK4 senses; dropped the degree-adverb 'quite/rather'.
cedict spans signboard/plate, brand, and playing/mahjong tile. At HSK4 the most useful everyday senses are 'card' (扑克牌) and 'sign/plate'; 'brand' (品牌) is also common, so glossed all three.
This entry is the là reading (colloquial), whose primary sense is 'to leave out / forget to bring / lag behind' — distinct from the luò reading (to fall/drop). Glossed per the given pinyin.
chosen: to leave out; miss; lag behind (the là reading)

reason: Many senses (draw out, smoke, shrink, sprout, whip). Primary HSK4 senses are 'to pull/draw out' and 'to smoke (cigarette)' — the two highest-frequency learner uses.

Senses split between 'weave/plait', 'edit/compile/compose', and 'fabricate/make up'. Chose the compile/weave/invent cluster as primary; all three are common at HSK4.
Means literal 'baby' and the affectionate 'darling/sweetie' term of endearment. Both common; glossed both, baby first as the core sense.
Pinyin is jiē (not jié), and at this reading the verb 'to tie/bear fruit' senses dominate; chose the core verb 'tie/knot' as most learnable primary, since the bound 'bear fruit' sense is narrower.
Spans 'penetrate/seep through', 'thoroughly (resultative complement)', and 'leak (a secret)'; chose the literal 'penetrate' + adverbial 'thoroughly' since both are the high-frequency HSK4 uses.
Two main HSK4 senses: medical 'to treat (a disease)' and political 'to govern/rule'; both common and distinct, so glossed both rather than forcing one.
Many senses (noisy, make trouble, fall ill, joke); chose the central 'make noise/make a fuss' which underlies the verb's everyday use.

reason: Both nominal 'row/series/column' and verbal 'arrange/list' are core; picked 'row; to list' as the most representative pairing for a learner.
Functions both as 'household/family' and as a measure word for households; included the measure-word note since it is a frequent HSK4 usage.
Senses 'return/go back' and 'belong to / be attributed to' are both high-frequency at HSK4; glossed both.

Spans 'fat (of person/animal)', 'fertile (soil)', and 'loose-fitting (clothes)'. At HSK4 the core sense a learner needs is the adjective 'fat/plump'; the 'loose-fitting clothes' and 'fertile' senses are secondary.
Two main senses: 'pine tree' (noun) and 'loose/to loosen/relax' (adj/verb). HSK4 prioritizes the loose/loosen sense (e.g. 放松 relax), so chose that over the pine-tree noun.
Spans 'to guard/defend' and 'to abide by/observe (rules)'. Both common at this level; chose to pair them since they share the core notion, leading with guard/defend.
Reads jiàng = 'to drop/fall/lower' but also 'to surrender/subdue' (and xiáng for surrender). HSK4 needs the 'lower/descend/drop' sense (cf. 下降, 降落), so set aside the surrender sense.
Spans 'to climb/ascend (登山)' and 'to publish/record/enter (登记, 登报)'. The physical climb sense is the primary HSK4 meaning; set aside the publish/register sense.
Many senses: pull/draw, attract, lead/guide, cite. Bundled the three most useful HSK4 senses (pull, attract, lead) as close senses.

reason: Verb with many senses; at HSK4 the core senses are dodging out of the way and a sudden flash/sparkle (lightning). Chose these two; set aside surname Shan, sprain/pull a muscle, and internet-slang saccharine.
Read as sàn (4th tone) per input. Primary intransitive sense at this level is to scatter/disperse/break up. Set aside the sǎn readings (loose, scattered, powdered medicine) and coll. 'to sack'.
Two readings: huá (row, scratch, strike a match, worthwhile) and huà (delimit, plan, transfer, stroke). Input pinyin is huá, so chose row/scratch. Set aside the huà senses and 'worth it'.
Concrete noun with sewing-needle, pin, and medical-injection senses. Kept needle + injection as the two most useful at HSK4; set aside pin and stitch.

means literal 'eldest brother' but also a polite address for a peer man and 'gang leader/boss'. At HSK4 the kinship sense is primary.

Senses: block up generally, traffic jam (堵车), choked-up emotion, literary 'wall', classifier. The everyday HSK4 use is blocking/traffic jam.
Verb 'to sink/submerge' vs adjective 'heavy/deep'. Both common; chose verb 'sink' as primary with adjective 'heavy' as second sense to triangulate.

Input pinyin 'chě' is the gōngchě-notation musical-note reading, a rare specialized sense. The HSK-4 learner needs the everyday reading chǐ = the unit of length / ruler, which is the dominant primary sense; corrected pinyin accordingly.

reason: cedict spans meals (伙食), companion/partner, group, and classifier. Chose the social 'group/partner/companion' sense as primary for the learner over the 'meals' abbreviation and the classifier function.

cedict gives both transitive 'to defeat (someone)' and intransitive 'to lose/fail'. For a learner the more common and safer primary sense is intransitive 'to be defeated; to fail'; transitive 'defeat sb' set aside.

cedict gives surname Jiang plus 'river/Yangtze'. As a vocabulary card the common-noun sense 'river (large)' is the right primary.
Senses span '(stage) lighting' and general 'light/lamplight'. Chose the general everyday sense over the theatre-specific one.
Multiple senses; at HSK4 the everyday meaning a learner needs is greeting/calling out, not the secondary 'to inform/take care of' senses.
Span of senses (tide / damp / trendy / coll. inferior). 'Damp, moist' is the primary adjectival sense at HSK4 (e.g. 潮湿); 'tide' is a noun sense and 'trendy/inferior' are colloquial.
Resultative with two readings (to defeat / to be defeated). Active 'to defeat' is the primary, more frequent learner sense.
Bound form with several senses (remit, converge, exchange, collect). At HSK4 the most useful primary sense for a learner is the money/remittance sense, common in 汇款/汇率.
Cedict lists visit/lock door/marry into wife's family; the dominant modern sense is visiting or coming to one's home (e.g. 上门服务).
Single char spanning surname Yan, tight/sealed, and strict/stern. The HSK4 adjective sense is strict/severe.
Means pull open/apart and also widen a gap/increase distance. Primary literal sense for the level is pulling open/apart, with the gap-widening sense noted.
cedict lists xiǎn (few/rare) and xiān (fresh); at HSK 4 the productive sense is xiān 'fresh', the reading learners need.
Primary literal sense is the melon/gourd family; the slang 'piece of gossip' sense is set aside as non-primary for HSK 4.
Spans linguistic 'pronunciation/speech sounds' and tech 'voice message/voice chat'. Chose pronunciation as primary with voice-message as common modern second sense.
cedict lists a Taiwan place name (Shulin) first, but at HSK4 the common-noun sense is what a learner needs.
alternatives: Shulin (place name in New Taipei City)

reason: Single character spanning several senses; at HSK4 the learner needs the two core senses: bestowing a reward and appreciating/admiring (beauty). Chose to combine both rather than the literary 'give to an inferior' sense.
cedict gives 'to recommend' and 'to introduce from outside'; the primary modern sense (import technology/talent/methods from outside) is far more common and HSK-relevant than 'recommend'.
Literal 'recharge a battery' is the primary HSK4 sense; the figurative 'recharge oneself / update skills' is secondary and dropped for button brevity.
Two senses: train/transport delay vs. 'a bit later' (晚一点). At HSK4 the transport-delay noun/verb sense is the standard textbook meaning, so primary is 'be late/delayed (of trains)'.

cedict leads with the pejorative 'arrogant idler/show-off' sense (dàyé), but the everyday colloquial kinship/respect sense (dàye, neutral tone) — father's elder brother / respectful term for an older man — is what a learner needs. Chose the kinship/respect sense.
chosen: father's elder brother; respectful address for older man

reason: Spans literal 'sway/rock' and figurative 'waver (in resolve)/destabilize'. At HSK4 the figurative resolve sense is dominant; kept both the figurative ('waver') and a hint of the transitive 'shake/destabilize'.
Multiple senses; chose 'central heating' as the primary everyday sense a learner needs (apartment/room heating), over 'heater' device or literal 'warm air'.

cedict lists 'second-hand', 'indirectly acquired', and 'assistant'. Chose the dominant common sense 'second-hand' (used goods); set aside the figurative 'indirect (information)' and the rare 'assistant' sense.
cedict spans 'roll call' and 'to name/single out sb (for praise or criticism)'. At HSK4 the everyday classroom sense (roll call) is what a learner needs first.
alternatives: to mention/single out sb by name
to call sb out by name

reason: Primary literal botanical sense vs several figurative ones (burst with joy, spring up everywhere). At HSK4 the literal 'to bloom' is the needed sense.
Concrete weather sense (temperature drop / cooling) vs figurative 'decline of interest or activity'. Weather sense is primary for HSK4.

cedict gives both a precise kinship term (father's elder brother's wife) and the common affectionate address for an older woman; at HSK4 the general 'auntie/older woman' sense is what a learner needs.
chosen: auntie / affectionate term for an older woman

reason: literal 'set meal' is the primary HSK4 sense; the figurative 'service/product package' (e.g. phone plan) is secondary but common, so included as a short second gloss.
cedict lists a derogatory political sense (Falun Gong practitioner), but the primary HSK-4 sense a learner needs is the concrete noun 'wheel'
Two readings: dǎochē 'to change buses/trains' vs dàochē 'to reverse a vehicle'. Chose the more common everyday-driving sense dàochē 'back up a car'

Term commonly covers e-bikes/scooters and electric cars; at HSK4 the everyday referent is the electric two-wheeler, but the literal/general sense is electric vehicle. Chose the general gloss with e-bike note to stay accurate to common usage.
Can mean a fashion/photo model (person) or an artist's/posing model; primary everyday sense is fashion model.
Polysemous: primary noun sense 'ghost/devil' is what an HSK 5 learner needs; suffix and 'crafty' senses set aside.
Bound form spanning wish/hope and willingness; chose the two core senses 'to wish' + 'willing' a learner meets in 愿意/祝愿.
Spans 'transport/move' and 'luck/fate'; at HSK 5 the salient standalone sense is luck (运气), so chose that over the verb 'transport'.
Core verb 'shoot/launch'; allude-to and chemistry-prefix senses set aside as peripheral for this level.
Multiple senses incl. tone/reputation/classifier; chose the everyday noun 'sound/voice' as the primary sense for this level.
Spans poison/narcotics/cruel. At HSK5 the core noun/adjective sense 'poison; poisonous' is what a learner needs; narcotics and 'malicious/cruel' are secondary.
cedict spans 'to sign', 'visa', 'label/tag', 'bamboo divination stick'. The dominant modern verb sense a learner needs is 'to sign'.
Spans price/value, chemistry valence, literary 'messenger/servant', and grammatical particle. The everyday bound-form sense is 'price; value'.
Spans 'emit/give off', 'to brave/risk', 'reckless', 'to feign'. Two most useful senses kept together: emitting (smoke/steam) and braving/risking.
Spans gift, rite/ceremony, propriety/etiquette. At HSK5 the two practical senses are 'etiquette/courtesy' and 'gift'; abstract 'rite' set aside.
Spans 'piece together', 'pool/share', 'risk all/go all out', 'to spell'. Kept the literal 'piece together' plus the common 'spell' sense; the colloquial 'go all out' set aside.
alternatives: to go all out; risk all

reason: Spans adverb 'certainly/will' and bound 'must'. Chose the modal-adverb sense ('must; certainly'), the primary HSK-5 usage in 必须/必然 contexts.

Cedict lists surname, 'stand', 'set up/establish', and 'at once'. Chose the concrete verb senses 'stand; set up' as the core learner meaning, dropping surname and the adverbial 'immediately' (立刻) reading.
chosen: стоять; ставить / to stand; set up

reason: Both verb 'to harm' and noun 'harm/calamity'. Gave both since they share one core sense; led with the verb, the more frequent HSK-5 usage (害人, 害怕 base).
'slippery/smooth' (adj), 'slip/slide' (verb), and figurative 'sly'. Chose literal slippery + slide; set aside the pejorative 'cunning' sense as secondary for this level.
alternatives: sly; cunning; not to be trusted

reason: Two distinct senses: literal 'turn one's head/round' and temporal 'later, by and by'. Kept both compactly; literal sense led as the primary learner meaning.
chosen: обернуться; попозже / turn one's head; later

reason: Two readings: dā (答应 'agree/respond') vs dá ('answer'). Input pinyin is dā (bound form). Glossed the answer/respond sense common to both, keeping pinyin dā as given.
Primary noun 'bear' plus colloquial 'to scold' / 'weak, incapable'. Chose the animal noun as the dominant HSK-5 sense; coll. verb/adj senses set aside.
cedict spans 'scissors' (tool/noun) and several verb senses (cut, exterminate, edit video). For HSK5 the core everyday sense is the verb 'to cut with scissors'; chose verb sense.
cedict lists 'one side/other/different' plus the technical 'lateral component of a character'. Learner-relevant primary sense is spatial 'side/beside'; chose that over the radical-naming meaning.
cedict spans 'hall/living room/office/provincial government department'. At HSK5 the concrete everyday senses (hall, living room) are primary; chose those over the government-department sense.
cedict spans literal spray and a slang 'criticize harshly online' sense plus crop-classifier uses; for HSK5 the concrete physical 'spray/spurt' is the primary sense a learner needs.
covers punish/penalize/fine; chose the broad 'punish; fine' pair as primary since the character generalizes beyond monetary fines.
can mean a music album or a special printed/broadcast collection; the music-album sense dominates modern usage at this level.
Bound morpheme with two senses; modern productive sense (society/agency, as in 社会/旅行社) is what an HSK5 learner needs, not the archaic god-of-the-land sense.

Many senses (verb to press/sandwich, noun clip/folder, adjective lined/double-layered). Chose the core jiā verb 'to clamp/press from both sides' plus the common noun 'clip' as the primary learner sense.
chosen: to clamp/press from both sides; clip

reason: Spans 'buying and selling/transaction' and the colloquial 'shop; store'. Chose the literal trade/business sense as primary for the level.
Several uses: 'all right/enough!' interjection vs '(rhetorical) possible'. Chose the high-frequency colloquial interjection 'enough!/all right!' as primary.
Polysemous: verb 'to fan' (shān) vs noun 'fan' (shàn, classifier for doors). Input pinyin is shān, the verb reading, so chose the verb sense as primary for this card.
Wide range of senses; for HSK5 the core concrete sense is physical 'pull out/up'. Set aside figurative/extended senses.
Two main senses: 'to mention/bring up' (speech) and literal 'to lift/raise'. At HSK5 the more common collocational use is 'mention', chosen as primary.
Several senses: 'quit/give up (a habit)', 'guard against/admonish', 'ring (finger)', Buddhist precept. Chose the everyday verb senses (quit; guard against) over the noun 'ring' and religious term.

Input pinyin 'yǎn' is the rare Kangxi-radical reading (cliff radical 广). As a standalone HSK-5 word 广 is read guǎng meaning 'wide/broad/extensive' (cf. 广场, 广告, 广大). A learner card needs the actual word sense, so I authored guǎng 'wide; broad'.
alternatives: yǎn — 'house on a cliff' radical (radical-name reading only)

reason: Spans literal 'to be finished' and the colloquial exclamation 'oh no / we're done for'. The colloquial idiomatic sense is what makes this a distinct HSK-5 vocabulary item beyond plain 完, so I led with it plus a register note.
Multiple discourse uses: contrast ('actually/on the contrary'), concession, and impatient 'why don't you'. Chose the core contrastive/concessive sense most needed at HSK 5.
cedict lists struggle-to-get-free (zhēngzhá) vs make money. At HSK5 the standalone high-frequency sense is the colloquial 'earn money' (zhèng); chose that with the matching tone.
alternatives: to struggle to get free (zhēng, in 挣扎)

reason: Spans suspend/time-out/pause. Chose the general 'suspend; pause' covering both meeting/sports time-out and media pause as the primary learner sense.
Two functions: 'that's fine / may as well' vs reduplicated 'whether...or...'. Chose the concessive 'and that's fine / may as well' as the primary single-occurrence sense for a learner.
Holds/grasp vs classifier 'a handful'. Chose the verb 'to hold; to grasp' as primary at HSK5.

Spans literal 'up/down', 'top and bottom', social hierarchy, and the approximation sense '...or so'. Chose the literal spatial sense plus the common 'about/approximately' use a HSK5 learner most needs.
chosen: верх и низ; около / up and down; about

reason: cedict lists offer/present/dedicate/donate/show plus archaic 'worthy person'. Chose the core verb sense of offering/dedicating, set aside 'show/display' and the archaic noun.
Means specifically the guqin zither but also 'musical instrument in general'. Chose qin/zither as the primary, noting general-instrument sense aside.
Two senses: bulk purchasing (e.g. of grain by a state body) and corporate acquisition/takeover. Both relevant at HSK5; combined both concisely.
chosen: скупать; поглощать (фирму) / to buy up; acquire

reason: Spans noun 'dregs/draff' and adjective 'rotten/messy/ruined'. At HSK5 the learner needs the common exclamatory/predicative sense 糟糕 'terrible, in a mess', so chose the adjective sense.
cedict lists key/button, chemical bond, and linchpin. The everyday HSK5 sense is the keyboard/mouse key/button; chemical bond is a specialized science sense.
Includes archaic 'enclosure' and the main 'idle/leisure/unoccupied' senses. Chose the productive modern adjective sense (free/idle) over the obsolete noun.
Two pronunciations/senses: zhuǎnxiàng 'change direction/stance' and a colloquial 'get lost'. Chose the primary literal-plus-figurative 'turn/change direction' which covers the main usage.
Both the noun 'drum' and verb 'to drum/rouse/bulge'. Chose the concrete noun 'drum' as primary with verb as secondary, since the noun is the core HSK meaning.

Spans literal 'gall bladder' and figurative 'courage/guts'. At HSK5 the figurative sense (courage) is the more common standalone usage, but the anatomical sense is the base meaning; led with both so the learner triangulates.
Can be verb 'to deposit money' or noun 'bank savings/deposit'. The noun sense (savings/deposit) is the primary dictionary head and most frequent; kept verb as secondary.
Literal 'depth' (measurement) vs figurative 'profundity/advanced stage'. Chose literal depth as the primary sense a learner needs first.
Word spans a verb sense (divide into) and noun/finance senses (profit split, percentage). For HSK5 the core need is the everyday verbal 'divide into'.
Has a literal verbal sense (panic) and a colloquial degree use after 得 (unbearably/terribly). Primary HSK5 sense is the emotional 'panic; be flustered'.
Several senses (raise head / emerge figuratively / commerce account-name). At HSK5 the concrete physical sense is what learners need; figurative and commercial senses set aside.
Spans religious worship and colloquial 'week'/'Sunday'. Colloquial 'week' is the most frequent everyday sense, but worship is the literal/primary meaning; glossed both compactly.
Bound form spanning verb 'to blame' and noun 'resentment/grudge'. Both common; kept both to triangulate the morpheme's sense.
Means a geographic area/region around a reference point, not a literal belt. Chose 'area; region' over the misleading literal reading.
Cedict spans adverbial 'overall/totally' and statistical noun 'population'. At HSK5 the learner needs the common adjectival/adverbial 'overall, total' sense, not the technical statistics term.
Senses: water spring, mouth of a spring, archaic word for coin. Primary modern sense is the natural water spring.
Functions as both noun 'incoming letter' and verb 'to send (us) a letter'. The noun sense is the primary, more frequent reading for a learner.
Pinyin given as hāi marks the interjection sense (surprise/regret) rather than ké (to cough), so glossed as an exclamation.
Has many senses (fork, cross, to intersect, to spread legs); for an HSK5 learner the concrete noun 'fork' plus the 'cross/X mark' sense are most useful.
Functions as a formal auxiliary before a disyllabic verb meaning 'apply the action to'; the literary 'moreover/in addition' sense set aside as secondary.
chosen: to apply (an action) to sth mentioned

reason: Spans 'lose money/deficit', 'luckily/thanks to', and an ironic 'fancy that'. For HSK5 the financial-loss sense (亏本, 亏损) is the core learner need; the adverbial 'luckily' and ironic uses are secondary/idiomatic.
Primary literal sense is geometric 'straight line'; the figurative 'sharply (rise/fall)' adverbial use (直线上升) is secondary and derived.
Means 'have not (yet/ever)'. Chose the 'never have' reading as primary since 不曾 most often negates a completed past action; 'not yet' is the close secondary reading.
cedict lists primary 'duck' and a slang 'male prostitute' sense; for HSK5 the everyday animal sense is what a learner needs.
Spans 'whole body / from head to toe', 'single person', and 'a suit of clothes'. Primary and most frequent learner sense is 'whole body / all over (covered in)'.
Verb-resultative meaning 'to give priority to / mainly rely on', often used as '...为主' = 'mainly/predominantly'. Chose the 'give priority to / predominantly' sense over literal 'rely on'.
Senses split between 'brittle/fragile' (objects) and 'crisp/crunchy' (food). Both are core; combined both as the dual primary sense rather than picking one.
Cedict lists 'give as present', 'repel', and posthumous-title senses; for HSK5 the everyday literary 'give as a gift' is the sense learners need.
alternatives: to bestow a posthumous honorary title

reason: Loanword spans the literal 'motor' and the common abbreviation for 摩托车 'motorbike'; the motorbike sense dominates real usage, so it leads with 'motor' as secondary.
Means both 'early or late / order' and the adverbial 'in succession, one after another'; the successive-order sense is the more frequent HSK5 usage and is chosen as primary.

Word spans literal 'moisture content' and figurative 'overstatement/padding'. At HSK5 the concrete literal sense (moisture in food/air) is what a learner needs first; the figurative sense is idiomatic and lower-priority.
Polysemous: 'generation', 'lifetime' (一辈子), and a literary classifier for people. Chose the core noun sense 'generation' as the primary anchor for the character at this level.
Two technical senses: data/energy 'output' and goods/services 'export'. Both common; led with 'output' (the more general everyday sense) but kept 'export' as complementary second gloss.

Word spans a calendar solar-term name (Rain Water) and the everyday sense of rainwater/rainfall. At HSK5 the concrete meteorological sense is what a learner needs, so the solar-term proper noun was set aside.
Spans the adverb 'mostly/for the most part' and the proper noun Dadu (Yuan-dynasty capital, modern Beijing). The functional adverb is the productive HSK5 sense; the historical place name set aside.
Real sense is 'legal system / rule of law'; cedict's 'made in France' is a homograph/secondary reading that is irrelevant for a learner. Chose the legal sense.

Spans military 'regiment commander' and civilian 'head of delegation / group-buy organizer'. Chose the canonical military/leader sense plus generic 'group leader' which covers delegation and modern group-buy usage at HSK5.
cedict lists a Chongqing place name first; that is a proper noun, not the learner sense. Chose the common adjective/noun 'longevity'.
Mainland sense is the sorghum spirit baijiu; a Taiwan-only abbreviation means 'white wine'. Chose the dominant mainland sense.
cedict spans literal 'enter a door' and figurative 'learn the basics/introduction'. At HSK-5 the figurative learning sense is the one learners need.
glosses split between concrete 'main part/bulk/body' and philosophical/legal 'subject/agent'. Chose the broadly applicable 'main part; subject' which covers the common HSK-5 usage.
cedict lists both literal 'peach blossom' and figurative 'love affair'; at HSK5 the concrete botanical sense is what a learner needs first.
cedict spans 'not easy/difficult' and the separate classical sense 'unchanging'; the common modern sense is 'not easy'.
literal 'both shores' vs. the political 'Taiwan and the mainland' reading; the literal geographic sense is the safe primary for a learner card and avoids political framing.
Verb that only appears in the frame 起到…作用; chose the bound 'play a role / have an effect' sense a learner needs over a literal gloss.
Spans 'size/height/stature'; for a person this is most often 'height/build', so led with that.
cedict gives two senses ('see repeatedly' vs narrative 'suddenly one sees'); the narrative 'and then one sees' is the dominant literary use at L5.
Literal 'sour/sweet/bitter/spicy' vs figurative 'joys and sorrows of life'; chose the idiomatic primary sense, noting literal in alternatives.
cedict lists 'to disinfect' first, but at HSK5 the computing sense (antivirus / kill a computer virus) is the primary modern usage learners encounter.
Refers both to the proper-noun platform Weibo and the generic 'microblog'. Kept both since the platform is the dominant real-world referent.
